name: Expectation vs Reality
description: Start with what people assume, then reveal the harsher or stranger reality.
example_hooks: Ancient Rome was not what movies showed you.
Space is not empty. It is violent.
The future looks clean. It may not feel simple.

name: POV Survival
description: Put the viewer inside the world and make each slide reveal what they would face next.
example_hooks: You wake up in Rome. Bad idea.
You landed on the wrong planet.
Your future apartment knows too much.

name: Strange Detail
description: Use one vivid detail as the thread, then reveal why it matters.
example_hooks: Rome's streets would hit your nose first.
This planet may rain sideways.
One tiny upgrade could change childhood.

name: What If Scenario
description: Frame a speculative question, then follow consequences step by step.
example_hooks: What if Earth had two moons?
What if school had AI teachers?
What if Mars became affordable?

name: Myth vs Truth
description: Open with a common misconception, then replace it with a sharper grounded reality.
example_hooks: Romans did not all live like Caesar.
Black holes are not cosmic vacuum cleaners.
Gladiator movies skipped the smell.

name: Hidden Cost
description: Show the attractive surface, then reveal the price paid underneath.
example_hooks: Rome's marble had a street-level cost.
A perfect future may need ugly tradeoffs.
Space travel has a brutal bill.

name: Timeline Compression
description: Compress a long process into a fast sequence of vivid turning points.
slide_structure: Start with the final shocking scale
example_hooks: Rome's daily life ran on old problems.
A star's death can become a city-sized monster.
The next century could arrive unevenly.
